research_question: In adults aged 18-74 years with treatment-resistant unipolar depression, what is the effectiveness of psychological therapies (as monotherapy or adjunct to antidepressants) compared to antidepressants, other psychological therapies, or attentional controls, in terms of depressive symptom reduction, treatment compliance, response/remission rates, social functioning, quality of life, economic outcomes, and adverse effects?

comparison.name: Comparison
exclude: active pharmacological control without psychological component

low agreement: selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor (1/3 models)
